metadata.name: praetor
metadata.summary: Automatic Generation of Provenance from Python3 Scripts

modules.name: praetor.python_builtin_wrap_templateGen
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
coding: utf-8

functions.name: praetor.python_builtin_wrap_templateGen.escape_forbidden_characters
documentation:
Escape forbidden characters in input string in accordance with PROV-N
\:param in_string: input string to escape characters within
\:return: modified in_string with escaped characters

functions.name: praetor.python_builtin_wrap_templateGen.createActivity
documentation:
Creates the activity and agent string for the PROV-N code and the relations between them, corresponding to the
function and python code, respectively
\:param activity: Name of function
\:param agent: Type of agent (typically software agent)
\:return: PROV-N format string for agent, activity and relations

functions.name: praetor.python_builtin_wrap_templateGen.createActivityStarted
documentation:
Creates the activity and agent string for the PROV-N code and the relations between them, corresponding to the
function and python code, respectively. For the case when a function was started by another
\:param activity: Name of function
\:param agent: Type of agent (typically software agent)
\:return: PROV-N format string for agent, activity and relations

modules.name: praetor.decorate
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
coding: utf-8

functions.name: praetor.decorate.findAll
documentation:
Sub-routine used to find import locations
\:param inString: input string to search
\:param findx: substring to look for
\:return: all start locations of findx

functions.name: praetor.decorate.find_matching
documentation:
Find lines which import modules specified within the praetor settings file
\:param importLines: Lines where import substring is present
\:param modSearch: List of modules to search for
\:return: Lines of target file which import the modules in modSearch

functions.name: praetor.decorate.module_string
documentation:
Creates the code to wrap imported modules and or functions
\:param mod: Module name
\:return: Python code to add to decorated file

functions.name: praetor.decorate.extract_commented_code
documentation:
Function to extract all code hidden behind comments
\:param line: String line of code
\:return: String of comments code or None if there are none

functions.name: praetor.decorate.get_indentation
documentation:
Function to return the indentation of any line of python code
\:param line: String of line to check
\:return: Numerical representation of indent

functions.name: praetor.decorate.add_fake_functions
documentation:
Reads an input script, searches for commented keywords, generates and inserts fake functions into the
decorated version of the script to record tailored provenance
\:param exampleScript: String of target python script
\:param loc_key: Keyword to trigger fake generation
\:return: original script with fake function additions as a string

functions.name: praetor.decorate.decorateFile
documentation:
Function to create a decorated version of a python script which wraps the desired imported modules/functions
and the functions defined within the script in order to record provenance. It also adds imports to required
modules and inserts fake functions based upon user input comment-hidden commands.
\:param exampleScript: Target script to decorate
\:param modules: List of target modules to decorate
\:param wrap_open: Whether to wrap pythons built-in open function
\:param global_tracking: Whether global variables should be tracked
\:return: Decorated version of exampleScript

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.memory
documentation: record allocated RAM per code segment - True/False

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.wrap_open
documentation: record provenance for pythons open function - True/False

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.build_prov
documentation: merge all recorded provenance files - True/False

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.flatten_prov
documentation: requires provtoolbox installation, removes duplicates and shrinks

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.global_tracking
documentation: option to record global variables throughout the code

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.provenance_directory
documentation: location for generated provenance storage

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.modules
documentation: list of python modules/functions to record provenance for - use the name assigned to it in python

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.blacklist
documentation: list of function to not include provenance for

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.staticmethods
documentation: leave blank unless there are complaints about static methods

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.json_file
documentation: name of json file. No need to change, it's defined here as many files need access to it

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.run_dir
documentation: os.getcwd()

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.unique_string
documentation: identifies func start/end in logs, must not be present in the logs otherwise

variables.name: praetor.praetor_settings.max_value_length
documentation: maximum no og chars for a value before it is stored separately from the provenance

functions.name: praetor.convert2prov.combine_prov
documentation:
Combines the output of praetor code to produce a singular provenance file.
\:param json_file: Bindings file name from praetor, usually named full_json.json
\:param agent_json_file: Bindings file name from praetor for agent data, usually named agent_json.json
\:param template_dir: Directory name where prov templates were generated
\:param out_prov: Name of the combined provenance file
\:return: One provenance file produced named out_prov which contains all information from the run

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_neo.create_conn
documentation:
Uploads provenance file to neo4j database and establishes a connection to it
\:param prov_file: Provenance file to upload
\:param add: Address of neo4j database
\:param usr: username for neo4j
\:param password: password for neo4j
\:param name: name of database to upload to
\:param scheme: uri scheme
\:param uri: uri to connect to neo4j
\:return: Connection to neo4j database to be used for queries

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_neo.query_handler
documentation:
Passes queries to Neo4j
\:param conn: Connection to Neo4j
\:param query_string: Query to pass
\:return: query results in a pandas dataframe

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_neo.clear_data
documentation:
Deletes all data from target neo4j graph database
\:param conn: connection to target graph
\:return:

modules.name: praetor.add_quality
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
coding: utf-8

modules.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
coding: utf-8

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.remove_name_spaces
documentation:
Remove namespaces from values in the provenance
\:param string: Input string to remove prefixes from
\:param name_spaces: Namespaces to remove
\:return: input string minus namespaces

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_handler
documentation:
Sends query to triple store
\:param query:
\:return: response of query

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_handler_histograms
documentation:
Sends query to triple store
\:param query:
\:return: response of query

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.upload_provenance
documentation:
Function for uploading provenance files to the database
\:param file_name: name of provenance file to upload
\:return: Name of the graph in the database

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.convert_to_datetime_exception
documentation:
Converts a string representation of datetime to a datetime object - temperamental
\:param datetime_str:
\:return: datetime object
def convert_to_datetime(datetime_str):
datetime_str = datetime_str[:-6]
time = datetime.strptime(datetime_str, '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f')
return time

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.result_to_df
documentation:
Converts a response from the database to a pandas dataframe
\:param response: response to convert
\:return: pandas dataframe format response

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.user_defined_query
documentation:
Function to pass a SPARQL query to the database
\:param query: string SPARQL query
\:return: dataframe of the results

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.object_histogram
documentation:
Creates a histogram for the potential values for parameters or runtime metrics within one or more provenance datasets
\:param pipeline_list: list of provenance files to upload and query
\:param object_name: Name (role) of the parameter or name of the function to investigate
\:param object_type: can be 'entity' or 'activity'
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: Data frame of the results and histogram plots

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_activity
documentation:
Queries the database for all activities within it and returns their names, ID, start/end times and memory usage
\:param pipeline: the name of the graph to query
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: results from the query

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_started_individual
documentation:
A query to return all functions that were started by a target function
\:param pipeline: name of the graph to query
\:param activity: name of the activity
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for query
\:return: The identifier of each function started by the target activity

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_was_started_by_individual
documentation:
A query to return all functions that started the target function
\:param pipeline: name of the graph to query
\:param activity: name of the activity
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for query
\:return: The identifier of each function that started the target activity

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_started
documentation:
A query to return all functions that were started by another
\:param pipeline: name of the graph to query
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for query
\:return: The identifier of each function started by another

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_input
documentation:
A query to find all inputs within a provenance dataset, the results show their ID, role, and value
\:param pipeline: the name of the graph to query
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for query
\:return: The identifier, role, and value of every entity used as an input

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_output
documentation:
A query to find all outputs within a provenance dataset, the results show their ID, role, and value
\:param pipeline: the name of the graph to query
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: The identifier, role, and value of every entity used as an output

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_output_individual
documentation:
> A query to find all outputs for a target function within a provenance dataset, the results show their ID, role,
>
> : and value

```
param pipeline

name of the graph to query

param activity

name of the activity

param prefixes

prefixes required for query

return

The identifier, role, and value of every entity used as an output in target function
```

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_input_individual
documentation:
A query to find all of a specific input within a provenance dataset, the results show their ID, role, and value
\:param pipeline: the name of the graph to query
\:param entity: role of an entity to search (name of the entity in python)
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: The identifier, role, and value of every entity used as an input

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_quality
documentation:
A query to extract quality metrics from provenance datasets
\:param pipeline: the name of the graph to query
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: All values and metrics of quality contained within the provenance and what they are attached to

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_resources_used
documentation:
Calculates the total memory and time for a function or a pipeline as a whole
\:param pipeline: name of the graph to query
\:param activity: name of the activity
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for query
\:return: total time (s) and memory (MB)

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_av_mem
documentation:
Determine all function which had a greater than average memory consumption
\:param pipeline: Name of pipeline to query
\:param prefixes: Prefixes required for query
\:return: Names of all functions with greater than average memory usage

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.query_activity_source
documentation:
Query to find all activities belonging to a particular source, typically either main or a module name
\:param pipeline: pipeline name to query
\:param module: name of module
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for query
\:return: All activities that belong to the target module

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.forward_search
documentation:
Function to find the n next objects in the provenance that are connected to object_id
\:param pipeline: name of provenance graph to query over
\:param object_id: id of object for the start of the search
\:param n_itter: number of connections from initial object to explore
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: dataframe of all objects connected to the object_id over n connections

functions.name: praetor.praetor_analysis_rdf.backward_search
documentation:
Function to find the n previous objects in the provenance that are connected to object_id
\:param pipeline: name of provenance graph to query over
\:param object_id: id of object for the start of the search
\:param n_itter: number of connections from initial object to explore
\:param prefixes: prefixes required for the query
\:return: dataframe of all objects connected to the object_id over n connections

modules.name: bin.add_quality
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
coding: utf-8

modules.name: praetor.genBindings
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
coding: utf-8

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.md5
documentation:
Function to create hash codes that represent data, files, etc.
\:param fname: Name of file to read and hash
\:return: Hash code specific to file fname

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.get_agent_string
documentation:
Create unique id for the agent of a pipeline and get the version of python and all imported modules
\:param modules: List of module names imported
\:return: Unique id for the agent and list of versions for modules

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.get_modules
documentation:
Function to create the agent_json.json file, including creating a unique id for the agent, determining all modules
imported, find their versions, structure and input all infroamtion into agent_json.json
\:return: agent_json.json

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.gen_identifier
documentation:
General utility function to create prov ids for python objects
\:param variable: Value of object to create id for
\:param naming_template: Name related to the object in question to include in the id
\:return: id for target object

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.escape_forbidden_characters
documentation:
Escape forbidden characters in input string in accordance with PROV-N
\:param in_string: input string to escape characters within
\:return: modified in_string with escaped characters

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.find_type
documentation:
If possible, return the type of input value and convert it to string
\:param value: Input value to type
\:param praetor_rec: switch to turn off provenance recording
\:return: String value, type of value

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.reduce_lists
documentation:
Sub-rountine for un-nesting lists if they are present
\:param in_list: Nested input list
\:return: Flattened version of in_list

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.get_default_args
documentation:
Extract the source code for a wrapped function
\:param decorated: Function source code including decorator
\:return: Function source code excluding decorator

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.find_dict_differences
documentation:
Used to find the differences between two dictionaries of global variables
\:param dict1: initial global variable dictionary
\:param dict2: updated global variable dictionary
\:return: Items in dict2 that are not in dict1

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.track_globs
documentation:
Find changes in global variabes between and old set and a freshly generated one
\:param initial_globals: Set of global variables to compare to
\:return: Differences between current and previous global variables

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.provcall
documentation:
Consumes as input the various parameters and metrics surrounding a function execution and writes them into a json
file as bindings, ready to combine with a template and make provenance.
\:param inlist: List of input values
\:param outlist: List of output values
\:param template: Name of provenance template corresponding to the function
\:param longname: Full name of the function (String)
\:param startTime: Start time of execution (dateTime object)
\:param endTime: End time of execution (dateTime object)
\:param memUse: Average memory consumption during function (Bytes)
\:param json_name: String for key to identify function execution in json file
\:param json_out: Name of json file to write data to
\:param jsonDir: Directory where json_out is located
\:param maxValueLength: Max number of characters for an input/output before it is saved as a "big entity"
\:param module_name: Name of thing where the function is defined (either main or a module)
\:param global_inputs: Dictionary of global variables consumed by a function
\:param global_outputs: Dictionary of global variables produced by a function
\:return: Updates json_out with the above information for the target function execution

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.get_globals
documentation:
Creates a dictionary of all global variables present within the target pipeline, execluding those added by the
praetor code and built-in variables
\:return: Dictionary of global variables

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.create_template
documentation:
Create a PROV-N template for a target python function
\:param func: Target function object
\:param template_name: Name of PROV-N template to create
\:param template_name_started: Name of PROV-N template if the function was started by another
\:param function_name: Name of function
\:param filePath: Path where templates will be generated
\:param global_tracking: Whether to track global variables
\:return: Creates the appropriate template for the function and stores a copy of the source code

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.provWrap
documentation:
Wrapper to decorate a function with in order to record provenance
\:param func: Target function object to wrap
\:return: An altered version of func which records provenance

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.provWrapOpen
documentation:
Wrapper alter pythons built-in open function so that it records provenance
\:param func: The open function object (typically just open)
\:return: Altered functionality open which also records provenance

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.for_all_methods
documentation:
Applies a target decorator to a class, was used to apply the provWrap but is currently out of use
\:param decorator: Decorator to add to a class
\:return: Class with altered functionality to record provenance

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.for_all_methods_inherited
documentation:
Applies a target decorator to a class, was used to apply the provWrap but is currently out of use
\:param decorator: Decorator to add to a class
\:return: Class with altered functionality to record provenance

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.decorate_imported_function
documentation:
Function to apply a decorator to an imported function
\:param func: Target function object
\:param decorator: Decorator to apply
\:return: Altered function which now records provenance

functions.name: praetor.genBindings.decorate_all_in_module
documentation:
Decorate the functions within imported modules
\:param module: Module to decorate
\:param decorator: decorator to apply
\:return: The function of the selected module is altered to record provenance

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.find
documentation:
Find start locations of all instances of a substring within a string
\:param inString: String to search through
\:param findx: Substring to seach for
\:return: Start locations of all substring positions

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.escape_forbidden_characters
documentation:
Escape forbidden characters in input string in accordance with PROV-N
\:param in_string: input string to escape characters within
\:return: modified in_string with escaped characters

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.separateLists
documentation:
Similar to a ','.split(inList) function, but it also corrects for peculiarities in python parameter formatting
\:param inList: String representation of a list to separate
\:return: True list of separated variable names

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.separate_output
documentation:
Walks the AST of a python function in order to separate outputs into distinct objects where applicable
\:param in_string: Function source code as string to walk
\:return: List of outputs from function

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.variableSeparation
documentation:
Deconstructs function source code into key components such as name, inputs, and outputs
\:param inString: Function source code to separate
\:return: Dictionary of separated objects

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.createInputEntities
documentation:
Creates the input entity section of a PROV-N template specific to a python function
\:param inputList: List of names of the inputs to a function
\:param activity: Name of the function
\:param global_tracking: Whether global variables are being recorded
\:return: String defining the input entities and their relation to the function in PROV-N format

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.createActivity
documentation:
Creates the activity and agent string for the PROV-N code and the relations between them, corresponding to the
function and python code, respectively
\:param activity: Name of function
\:param agent: Type of agent (typically software agent)
\:return: PROV-N format string for agent, activity and relations

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.createActivityStarted
documentation:
Creates the activity and agent string for the PROV-N code and the relations between them, corresponding to the
function and python code, respectively. For the case when a function was started by another
\:param activity: Name of function
\:param agent: Type of agent (typically software agent)
\:return: PROV-N format string for agent, activity and relations

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.createOutputEntities
documentation:
Creates the output entity section of a PROV-N template specific to a python function
\:param outputList: List of names of the outputs to a function
\:param global_tracking: Whether global variables are being recorded
\:return: String defining the output entities and their relation to the function in PROV-N format

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.generateBundle
documentation:
Combines the PROV-N strings created by the other functions in this file and generates the full PROV-N template in
string format.
\:param function: List generated by praetor which includes the function name, inputs, outputs, etc.
\:param bundleStart: PROV-N format string which declares the start of the provenance document
\:param bundleEnd: PROV-N format string which declares the end of the provenance document
\:return: String format PROV-N template for target function

functions.name: praetor.praetorTemplates.globalBundle
documentation:
Function used only when global tracking is enabled. Takes the names, values, and IDs of global parameters and
generates PROV-N entity definitions for them as well as relations to the intended object already present in the
provenance
\:param parameter_key: List of keys of the parameters in the global dictionary
\:param parameter_value: List of values of the parameters in the global dictionary
\:param globalID: Prov ID generated for the collection of global parameters
\:param parameterID: List of Prov IDs generated for the global dictionary
\:param parameter_type: List of python types for objects in the global dictionary
\:return: String representation of the entity definitions and relations for global variables

modules.name: bin.praetor_run
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: bin.praetor_run.generate_pipeline_id
documentation:
Creates a unique id for a pipeline run which includes the praetor version
\:return: Unique id

functions.name: bin.praetor_run.escape_forbidden_characters
documentation:
Escapes special characters from an input string to fit PROV-N
\:param in_string: String to escape characters within
\:return: Original string with escaped characters

functions.name: bin.praetor_run.exclude_internals
documentation:
Patch to exclude modules used by praetor from provenance recording. This limits the capabilities but stops bugs.
A future solution would be to not record provenance when praetor is active, but that is difficult to determine.
Perhaps a link in the stack between last usage being praetor module and all subsquent being something that praetor
could have called?
\:param exclude: list of all possible internal modules
\:param modules: all user input modules
\:returns: refined list
